evals.prompt: My kids have been gaming way too much lately. I want to set weekdays to a max of 1 hour and weekends to 2 hours. They should also stop at 9pm every night and be able to start again at 7am. How do I configure this on their Switch? We're in the US (America/New_York timezone).
evals.expected_output: A complete sequence of CLI commands: set-timer-mode EACH_DAY_OF_THE_WEEK, then set-day-restrictions for each day of the week with correct --playtime-enabled/--max-playtime-minutes AND --bedtime-enabled/--bedtime-alarm-hour/--bedtime-end-hour flags, plus the correct timezone flag. All commands must be syntactically correct and include the required --bedtime-enabled/--bedtime-disabled flag on every set-day-restrictions call.
expectations: Uses set-timer-mode EACH_DAY_OF_THE_WEEK before setting per-day restrictions
All set-day-restrictions calls include either --bedtime-enabled or --bedtime-disabled
Bedtime alarm is set to hour 21 (9pm) when --bedtime-enabled is used
Bedtime end is set to hour 7 (7am) when --bedtime-enabled is used

evals.prompt: I want to check what games my kids played last month (April 2025) and how long each game was played. I have two Switches linked to my account - 'Switch #1' is for my older kid and 'Switch #2' is for the younger one. I want to see the monthly summary for Switch #2 first, then the list of games played on it.
evals.expected_output: monthly-summary for Switch #2 with --year 2025 --month 4 and --format json. list-applications is not appropriate here since it only shows today's data. The markdown monthly-summary only shows totals and per-player totals — per-game data requires --format json to get the raw API payload.
expectations: Device name 'Switch #2' is passed correctly (quoted if needed)
Uses --format json because the prompt asks for per-game data, which the markdown output does not expose

evals.prompt: It's a Friday night and I want to give my kid 45 extra minutes of playtime tonight as a treat. Also, they've been trying to play games that are rated for older teens - I want to lock the Switch down to only allow content appropriate for younger children. How do I do both of those things?
evals.expected_output: add-extra-time command with 45 minutes, and set-content-restriction CHILDREN command.
expectations: Does not confuse add-extra-time argument order (minutes is the last positional arg)
